prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-xTwJa8.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: choose event-driven vs direct calls architecture, define core types/interfaces, implement base notification service, create email adapter

parameters.summary: Unify notification system on event-driven architecture via EventBus, with pluggable NotificationAdapter interface for delivery channels
parameters.context: The existing codebase has two communication patterns: EventBus (for order:created) and CallbackRegistry (for order:status-changed). The notification service uses both inconsistently. The codebase comments explicitly flag this as a problem to fix. 11 more developers will build on this foundation.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is already the primary pattern in the codebase. It provides typed events, event logging for debugging, async handlers, and loose coupling. The adapter pattern on top allows adding new notification channels (email, webhook, SMS, in-app) without modifying the core service. The CallbackRegistry pattern will be eliminated for consistency.

alternatives.option: Direct service calls pattern — NotificationService called directly by OrderService/UserService
alternatives.reason_rejected: Creates tight coupling between business logic and notification delivery. Adding new notification triggers requires modifying calling services. Contradicts the existing EventBus architecture.
Explicitly flagged as problematic in existing code comments. Two patterns for the same concern increases cognitive load and makes it harder for 11 additional developers to know which pattern to use.

constraints: All notification adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls for notifications
The NotificationService subscribes to events and delegates to registered adapters
New notification channels are added by implementing NotificationAdapter and registering with NotificationService

parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */
parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are emitted
 * via EventBus. The NotificationService subscribes to these events
 * and routes them to the appropriate notification adapters.
 */

parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven via EventBus with pluggable NotificationAdapter interface
parameters.detail:
Implemented:
1. Architecture: Event-driven via EventBus (unified, replaced mixed CallbackRegistry pattern)
2. Core types in src/models/notification.ts: NotificationChannel, NotificationType, NotificationPriority, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationRecord
3. NotificationAdapter interface in src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts — all delivery channels must implement this
4. EmailAdapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts — stub implementation storing sent emails in memory
5. Refactored NotificationService to subscribe to ALL domain events via EventBus and route to registered adapters
6. Fixed OrderService to emit order:status-changed via EventBus instead of CallbackRegistry
7. Updated all tests — 72/72 passing, clean tsc compile

Constraints for other developers:
- ALL notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls
- ALL delivery channels must implement NotificationAdapter interface
- Register adapters via NotificationService.registerAdapter()
- New event types: add handler in NotificationService.registerEventHandlers()
- New channels: implement NotificationAdapter, register with service

parameters.summary: All notification adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface; all notifications flow through EventBus
parameters.detail:
Architectural constraints for the notification system:
1. Every delivery channel (email, webhook, SMS, in-app) must implement the NotificationAdapter interface from src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
2. All notifications must originate from domain events emitted via EventBus — no direct NotificationService.send() calls from business logic
3. NotificationService is the ONLY subscriber that handles event→notification routing
4. One adapter per channel; registering a second replaces the first
5. The CallbackRegistry pattern (src/utils/callback-registry.ts) is deprecated for notifications — do not use it for new notification flows

parameters.summary: CallbackRegistry (src/utils/callback-registry.ts) is now unused for notifications — do not add new notification flows using it
parameters.detail: The CallbackRegistry and statusChangeCallbacks export still exist in the codebase but are no longer used by OrderService or NotificationService. They were replaced by EventBus for consistency. The file can be kept for non-notification uses but should NOT be used for any notification-related flows.

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven architecture via EventBus

Replace mixed EventBus + CallbackRegistry pattern with a consistent
event-driven approach. All domain events now flow through EventBus,
and NotificationService routes them to pluggable NotificationAdapter
implementations.

- Add NotificationAdapter interface for delivery channel abstraction
- Add core notification types (NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, etc.)
- Add EmailAdapter stub implementing NotificationAdapter
- Refactor NotificationService to subscribe to all events via EventBus
  and delegate delivery to registered adapters
- Migrate OrderService status changes from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- Add handlers for user:created and user:updated events
- Update tests to match new architecture (72/72 passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..691b50d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification delivery
+ * channels must implement.
+ *
+ * Architecture constraint: Every notification channel (email, webhook, SMS,
+ * in-app) is implemented as an adapter conforming to this interface. Adapters
+ * are registered with NotificationService, which routes payloads to the
+ * appropriate adapter based on the payload's channel field.
+ *
+ * To add a new notification channel:
+ * 1. Add the channel name to NotificationChannel in src/models/notification.ts
+ * 2. Create a new adapter implementing this interface
+ * 3. Register the adapter with NotificationService via registerAdapter()
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification via this adapter's channel.
+   * Must return a NotificationResult indicating success or failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c771800
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model — core types for the notification system.
+ *
+ * Architecture: Event-driven via EventBus with pluggable adapters.
+ * - Services emit domain events (order:created, user:updated, etc.)
+ * - NotificationService listens to events and creates NotificationPayloads
+ * - Registered NotificationAdapters deliver notifications via their channel
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
+ * - All notifications flow through EventBus — no direct service calls
+ * - New channels are added by implementing NotificationAdapter
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Supported notification channels. Extend this union as new adapters are added.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'webhook' | 'in-app' | 'sms';
+
+/**
+ * Notification types corresponding to domain events.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order_confirmation'
+  | 'order_status_update'
+  | 'welcome'
+  | 'account_updated';
+
+/**
+ * Priority levels for notification delivery.
+ */
+export type NotificationPriority = 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
+
+/**
+ * The payload passed to notification adapters for delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification ID */
+  id: string;
+  /** The type of notification being sent */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** Delivery channel */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Recipient identifier (email address, user ID, webhook URL, etc.) */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Notification subject / title */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content */
+  body: string;
+  /** Priority level */
+  priority: NotificationPriority;
+  /** When the notification was created */
+  createdAt: Date;
+  /** Optional metadata for adapter-specific needs */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Result returned by a NotificationAdapter after attempting delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+  /** Whether delivery succeeded */
+  success: boolean;
+  /** The notification that was sent */
+  notificationId: string;
+  /** The channel used */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Error message if delivery failed */
+  error?: string;
+  /** Timestamp of the delivery attempt */
+  deliveredAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Stored notification record combining payload and delivery result.
+ */
+export interface NotificationRecord {
+  payload: NotificationPayload;
+  result: NotificationResult;
+}
